answerOptions.title: Facendo evaporare l'acqua contenuta nel latteEvoporating the water in the milk
Aggiungendo speciali batteriiBy adding special bacteria
Usando uno speciale tio di latteUsing a special type of milk

answerExplanation.text:
Lo yogurt viene creato aggiungendo batteri speciali. I batteri mangiano gli zuccheri e addensano il latte. Attraverso questo processo è possibile ottenere 1 litro di yogurt da 1 litro di latte.

Ecco perché lo yogurt ha un'impronta molto inferiore rispetto alla panna, al formaggio o al burro, che richiedono molto più latte. In generale si può dire che più grassi contiene un prodotto, più latte è stato utilizzatoYoghurt is created by adding special bacteria. The bacteria eat the sugars and thicken the milk. Through this process you can get 1 liter of yoghurt from 1 liter of milk.

That’s why yoghurt has a much lower footprint than cream, cheese, or butter, which require a lot more milk. Generally you can say the more fat a product contains, the more milk was used.

pages.subtitle: Prova questa ricetta indiana con lo yogurtTry this recipe from India with yoghurt!

pages.hometitle: Come viene prodotto lo yogurt a base vegetaleHow is plant-based yogurt made?
pages.title: I batteri presenti nello yogurt possono avere molti benefici per la saluteThe bacteria in yoghurt can have many health benefits ✨
Anche lo yogurt vegetale contiene questi batteri beneficiDoes plant-based yoghurt also include these beneficial bacteria? 🥣

answerExplanation.text:
Anche lo yogurt vegetale viene prodotto tramite fermentazione batterica. Questo lo rende ‘vero yogurt’ e non un sostituto elaborato.

Lo yogurt di soia è solitamente l'opzione più sana rispetto al cocco o all'avena (puoi controllare il Nutriscore). È ricco di proteine e di solito è fatto con ingredienti molto semplici: acqua, semi di soia, calcio e batteriPlant-based yoghurt is also created through fermentation with bacteria. This makes it ‘real yoghurt’ and not some processed replacement.

Soy yoghurt is usually the healthiest option compared to coconut or oat (you can check the Nutri score). It is high in protein and is usually made with very simple ingredients: water, soy beans, calcium, and bacteria!

dailyContent.notificationText: Quanta carne mangiavano i nostri nonniHow much meat did our grandparents eat? 🥩 - Recipe of the day: Leek and Lentil Quiche con porri e lenticchie

home.title: Quanta carne mangiavano i nostri nonniHow much meat did our grandparents eat?

pages.title: Oggi, nel mondo si mangia circa 5 volte più carne rispetto al 1961Today, the world eats around 5 times more meat than in 1961.

Nel complesso, la maggior parte di questo aumento si è verificato in Asia perché lì vivono molte persone. Ma cosa succederebbe se considerassimo il consumo di carne pro capiteOverall, most of this increase happened in Asia because many people live there. But what if we look at meat consumption per person?
Dove è aumentato maggiormente il consumo di carneWhere did meat consumption increase more?
pages.subtitle: pro capite dalper person since 1961

answerExplanation.text:
In Europa e Nord America il consumo di carne era già piuttosto elevato. E poi è ulteriormente aumentato di un terzo.

Il rapporto EAT Lancet ha calcolato quale ammontare pro capite di carne potrebbe essere sostenibile: 16 kg all'anno. Ciò significa che gli europei devono passare da mangiare carne più volte al giorno a mangiarla 2-3 volte a settimanaIn Europe and North America, meat consumption was already quite high. And then it further increased by one third.

The EAT Lancet report calculated what a sustainable of meat per person could look like: 16 kg per year. That means Europeans need to go from having meat multiple times a day, to eating meat 2-3 times a week.

pages.subtitle: Una quiche senza carne che convincerà qualsiasi amante della carneA meatless quiche that will convince any meatlover.

pages.title: Cosa succederebbe se tutti mangiassero come un americanoWhat would happen if everyone ate like an American?

answerOptions.title: Avremmo la pace nel mondoWe would have world peace 🇺🇸
Dovremmo tagliare tutte le foreste del mondoWe would have to cut all forests in the world 🪓
Non sarebbe possibile, la terra è troppo piccolaNot possible, Earth is too small 🌎

answerExplanation.text:
Se tutti mangiassero come gli Stati Uniti, il mondo sarebbe letteralmente troppo piccolo. Se tutti mangiassero come gli olandesi o i tedeschi, dovremmo tagliare TUTTE le foreste e usare ogni pezzetto di natura per la produzione alimentare.

D'altra parte, se tutti mangiassero come l'India avremmo bisogno della metà della terra che usiamo oggiin questo momentoIf everyone would eat like the US, the world would literally be too small. If everyone would eat like the Dutch or Germans, we would have to cut ALL forests and use every bit of nature for food production.

On the other hand, if everyone ate like India we would need half the land we use right now!

dailyContent.notificationText: Sai dove viene utilizzata la maggior parte della nostra acquaDo you know where most of our water is used? 💦 - Recipe of the day: Patate e pesto al fornoPotato and Pesto Bake

home.title: la maggior parte dell’acqua viene usata perMost water is used for…

pages.title: Anche se viviamo su un pianeta blu, l'acqua dolce è estremamente rara. La maggior parte è salata o racchiusa nel ghiaccioEven though we live on a blue planet, fresh water is extremely rare. Most of it is salty or locked away in ice 🧊
Per fornire a ogni essere umano acqua a sufficienza, dobbiamo usarla in modo più responsabileTo provide every human with enough water, we need to use it more responsibly.
Dove viene utilizzata la minore quantità di acquaWhere is the least amount of water used?
pages.subtitle: a livello mondialeworldwide

answerExplanation.text:
Di tutta l'acqua che preleviamo da fiumi, laghi o depositi sotterranei, il 70% viene utilizzato per l'agricoltura. Ciò significa che il nostro cibo svolge un ruolo importante nel prendersi cura dell’acqua.

In alcuni paesi europei, l’industria utilizza la maggior parte dell’acqua, mentre l’uso privato solitamente costituisce la quota più piccolaOf all the water we withdraw from rivers, lakes or underground storage, 70% is used for agriculture. That means our food plays an important role in taking good care of water.

In some European countries, industry uses the most water but household is usually the smallest share.

pages.subtitle: Le patate sono uno dei prodotti alimentari con la minore impronta idricaPotatoes are one of the food products with the lowest water footprint.

pages.hometitle: Quanta parte della nostra produzione alimentare dipende dalla pioggiaHow much of our food production is dependent on rain?
pages.title: Nonostante l'enorme quantità d'acqua utilizzata per l'irrigazione, gran parte del nostro cibo dipende anche dalla pioggia 🌧 La domanda è: quantaDespite using so much water for irrigation, a lot of our food is also dependent on rain 🌧 The question is: how much?
Cosa fornisce più ciboWhat provides more food?
pages.subtitle: a liello mondialeworldwide

answerExplanation.text:
Il 60% della nostra produzione alimentare dipende dalla pioggia. Ma con gli eventi meteorologici più estremi dovuti ai cambiamenti climatici, la produzione alimentare diventa meno prevedibile.

A volte sembra che gli esseri umani governino il mondo, ma è bene ricordare che dipendiamo completamente dalla natura60% of our food production is reliant on rain. But with more extreme weather from climate change, food production becomes less predictable.

It sometimes feels like humans run the world but it’s good to remember that we are completely dependent on nature.

dailyContent.notificationText: La biodiversità è importante. Ma perché?Biodiversity is important. But why? 🌼 - Recipe of the day: Beetroot and Pumpkin Curry alla zucca e barbabietola

home.title: Il fascino della diversitàThe beauty of diversity

pages.title: Immagina un campo pieno di fiori 🌼 Più colori e varietà ci sono, più bello èImagine a field full of flowers 🌼 The more colors and diversity, the more beautiful it looks!
La biodiversità non è solo bella da vedere, ma è anche importante per l'agricoltura Biodiversity is not only nice for the eye, but also important for farming 🧑‍🌾
La diversità in un'azienda agricola è importante perchéDiversity on a farm is important because it…

answerOptions.title: …rende l'agricoltura più efficientemakes farming more efficient
…richiede meno energia per essere raccoltorequires less energy to harvest

answerExplanation.text:
Se tutti i meli sono uguali e uno di essi si ammala, si ammalano tutti e non avremo più mele. Questo è un grosso rischio perché le malattie delle piante sono più probabili con il cambiamento climatico.

La diversità è una delle caratteristiche più importanti di un ecosistema sano. Crea anche sapori più unici, supporta le tradizioni alimentari locali e crea un paesaggio più belloIf all apple trees are the same and one of them gets sick, they all get sick and we don’t have any apples. This is a big risk because plant diseases are more likely with climate change.

Diversity is one of the most important features of a healthy ecosystem. It also creates more unique flavours, supports local food traditions and creates a more beautiful landscape 🙂

pages.subtitle: Un modo semplice per promuovere la diversità è cucinare piatti (a base vegetale) con tanti colori diversiA simple way to support diversity is cooking (plant-based) meals with lots of different colors!

pages.title: Una minore biodiversità può aumentare il rischio diLower biodiversity can increase the risk of…

answerExplanation.text:
Quando la diversità di animali e piante diminuisce, ad esempio sostituendo la foresta con una monocoltura, diventa più difficile per gli 'specialisti' (specie che dipendono da piante o ecosistemi specifici) sopravvivere e gli animali 'generalisti' come i ratti prosperano. Questi animali sono spesso portatori di virus pericolosi e, poiché la regolazione naturale delle malattie viene 'interrotta', diventa più facile per un virus diffondersi all'uomo.

 Trovare risposte false a questa domanda è stato davvero difficile perché la biodiversità migl iora quasi tuttoWhen the diversity of animals and plants goes down - for example replacing forest with a monoculture - it makes it harder for ‘specialists’ (species depending on specific plants or ecosystems) to survive and ‘generalist’ animals like rats thrive. These animals often carry dangerous viruses and as nature's own regulation of diseases is 'broken' it becomes easier for a virus to spread to humans.

Coming up with false answers for this question was really hard because biodiversity improves almost everything!

dailyContent.notificationText: I danni dei fertilizzanti chimici The damage of chemical fertilizer ☠️ - Recipe of the day: Torta di patate dolci e barbabietolaSweet Potato and Beetroot Tart

home.title: I danni dei fertilizzanti chimici The damage of chemical fertilizer

pages.title: I fertilizzanti chimici possono stimolare la crescita delle colture a breve termine Chemical fertilizer can boost the growth of crops in the short-term 🌱

Ma 'prende in prestito' questa produttività dal futuro perché danneggia il suolo ☠️. E questo non è l'unico problema.But it ‘borrows’ this productivity from the future because it damages the soil ☠️. And that’s not the only issue.
I fertilizzanti chimiciChemical fertilizer…

answerOptions.title: … causano il 14% di tutte le emissioni alimentaricauses 14% of all food emissions ☁️
… usano il 14% di tutta l’acqua dolceuses 14% of all fresh water 💦

answerExplanation.text:
Sentiamo spesso parlare delle emissioni derivanti dal trasporto degli alimenti, ma i fertilizzanti ne causano più del doppio.

La produzione di fertilizzanti richiede un'enorme quantità di energia. E quando vengono sparsi sui campi, i microrganismi presenti nel terreno li trasformano in protossido di azoto, aumentando ulteriormente le emissioni.

Spesso gli agricoltori usano anche troppi fertilizzanti, il che crea un grave inquinamento idrico.We often hear about the emissions of food transportation, but fertilizer causes more than double the emissions.

It costs an enormous amount of energy to manufacture fertilizer. And when it is spread on the field, micro-organisms in the soil convert it to laughing gas, adding even more emissions.

Often farmers also use too much fertilizer, which creates serious water pollution.

pages.subtitle: Come abbiamo già visto, i legumi fanno bene al terreno. Qui usiamo l'hummus come 'terreno' di queste deliziose torteLike we’ve seen before, legumes are good for the soil. Here we use hummus as the ‘soil’ of these delicious tarts.

pages.hometitle: Come possiamo ridurre l'impatto climatico dei fertilizzantiHow can we reduce the climate impact of fertilizer?
pages.title: I fertilizzanti chimici non sono del tutto dannosi e poco meno della metà della popolazione mondiale dipende da essiChemical fertilizer is not all bad and a little less than half of all people on Earth are dependent on it 🌍
Il problema principale è che spesso ne usiamo troppo. Ma ci sono delle soluzioni The main problem is that we often use too much. But there are solutions ✨

In un grande esperimento durato 10 anni, gli agricoltori cinesi sono riusciti ad aumentare i raccolti utilizzando MENO fertilizzanti In a big 10-year-long experiment, Chinese farmers were able to increase yields while using LESS fertilizer 🌾
Come ci sono riuscitiHow did they achieve this?

answerOptions.title: Tecnologia più avanzata per l'applicazione dei fertilizzanti Better technology to apply fertilizer 🚜

answerExplanation.text:
Formando gli agricoltori, hanno aumentato la resa di mais, riso e grano riducendo al contempo l'uso di fertilizzanti. Ciò ha ridotto l'impatto climatico del 14-22%!

 Ad esempio, hanno contribuito a migliorare i tempi di semina e, invece di applicare il fertilizzante tutto in una volta, lo hanno fatto in due dosi più piccole.By training farmers, they increased the yield of corn, rice, and wheat while reducing fertilizer use. This reduced the climate impact by 14-22%!

For example, they helped to improve the timing when crops are planted and instead of applying fertilizer all at once, doing it in two smaller batches.

dailyContent.notificationText: Come funziona l'impronta idrica How a ‘water footprint’ works 💦 - Recipe of the day: Minty Aubergine Wraps alle zucchine e menta

home.title: Come funziona l'impronta idrica How a ‘water footprint’ works

pages.title: L'acqua che usiamo per la doccia o l'irrigazione proviene da fiumi, laghi o falde acquifere sotterranee. Viene definita 'acqua blu' The water we use for showers or irrigation comes from rivers, lakes or underground storage. It’s called ‘blue water’ 🚿
Per l'agricoltura, dobbiamo anche considerare l'’acqua verde’ For agriculture, we also need to take a look at ‘green water’ 💦
Cos’è l’’acqua verde’What is green water?

answerOptions.title: Acqua utilizzata per sciogliere le particelle inquinanti Water used to dissolve polluting particles ☠️
Acqua immagazzinata nel terreno attraverso le precipitazioni Water stored in the soil through rainfall 🌧
Acqua di fusione dei ghiacciaiMelt water from glaciers 🧊

answerExplanation.text:
L'acqua blu viene prelevata direttamente da fiumi, laghi o serbatoi sotterranei. Questa è l'acqua utilizzata per le docce o l'irrigazione.

L'agricoltura ha un altro grande aspetto 'nascosto' dell'acqua che è altrettanto importante: l'acqua verde. Questa viene immagazzinata nel suolo attraverso le precipitazioni. Insieme creano l'impronta idrica di un prodotto alimentareBlue water is directly taken from rivers, lakes, or underground storage. This is the water used for showers or irrigation.

Agriculture has another large ‘hidden’ aspect of water that also matters: green water. This is stored in the soil through rainfall. Together they create the water footprint of a food product.

pages.subtitle: Gli avocado sono piante che necessitano di molta acqua, quindi abbiamo voluto creare dei wrap deliziosi senza bisogno di avocadoAvocados are very thirsty plants so we wanted to create delicious wraps without the need for avocado.

pages.hometitle: Perché dovremmo preoccuparci di quanta acqua piovana sia necessaria per un prodotto alimentareWhy should we care how much rainwater a food product requires?
pages.title: Se l'acqua verde proviene principalmente dalla pioggia, perché dovremmo preoccuparceneSo if green water mostly comes from rain, why should we care about it? 🌧
I prodotti con una bassa impronta idrica verde sono più sostenibili perchéProducts with a low green water footprint are more sustainable because:

answerOptions.title: Riduce il rischio di siccità It decreases the risk of droughts ☀️
Migliora la salute del suolo It increases soil health 🪱
Riduce l'inquinamento idrico It reduces water pollution ☠️

answerExplanation.text:
La maggior parte del nostro cibo viene prodotta grazie alla pioggia. Ma il cambiamento climatico rende il tempo più estremo, quindi fare affidamento sulla pioggia diventa più imprevedibile.

E se non piove, servono comunque 2170 litri d'acqua per far crescere l'erba e il mais per una bistecca. Quindi, a meno che non si utilizzi più acqua blu, non ci sarà nessuna bistecca. Ecco perché l'acqua verde è importante e perché mangiare cibi con una minore impronta idrica è più sostenibileThe majority of our food is produced with rain. But climate change makes the weather more extreme, so relying on rain becomes more unpredictable.

And if it doesn’t rain, you still need 2170 liters of water to grow the grass and corn for a steak. So unless you use more blue water there is no steak. This is why ‘green water’ matters and why eating foods with a lower water footprint is more sustainable.

dailyContent.notificationText: L'impronta dei pomodori in inverno The footprint of tomatoes in winter 🍅 - Recipe of the day: Chickpea Tajine di ceci con peschewith Peaches

home.title: L'impronta dei pomodori in inverno The footprint of tomatoes in winter

pages.title: I pomodori sono l'ortaggio più popolare in Europa 🍅. Sono un ortaggio estivo, ma ora si possono acquistare tutto l'annoTomatoes are the most popular vegetable in Europe 🍅. They’re a summer vegetable, but you can now buy them year-round.
Esistono diversi modi in cui i supermercati offrono pomodori durante i freddi mesi invernali There are several ways in which supermarkets offer tomatoes during cold winter months ❄️
Quale tipo di 'pomodoro invernale' ha l'impronta più elevataWhat type of ‘winter tomato’ has the highest footprint?

answerOptions.title: Confezionato in una lattina Packaged in a can 🥫
Coltivato in una serra riscaldata Grown in a heated greenhouse 🔥
Trasportato da un paese caldo Transported from a warm country 🚚

answerExplanation.text:
Naturalmente, il cibo di stagione è l'opzione migliore. Ma durante i mesi invernali, anche il cibo in scatola è una buona opzione. I pomodori in scatola vengono raccolti durante l'estate. Nella lattina rimangono freschi e sono spesso più gustosi dei pomodori provenienti da una serra.

 È facile sentirsi stressati dalla scelta con tutte queste opzioni. Basta tenere a mente: mangiare meno prodotti di origine animale è più importante che acquistare il pomodoro più sostenibileOf course, seasonal food is the best option. But during winter months, canned food is also a good option. Canned tomatoes are harvested during summer. In the can they remain fresh anda are often tastier than tomatoes from a greenhouse.

It’s easy to feel decision stress with all these options. Just keep in mind: eating fewer animal products is more important than buying the most sustainable tomato.

pages.subtitle: Questi ingredienti in scatola aiutano a creare un pasto delizioso a base di sedano rapa, una verdura 'dimenticata' durante i mesi invernaliThese canned ingredients help to create a delicious meal around the celeriac, a ‘forgotten’ vegetable for the winter months.

pages.hometitle: Quali sono i benefici dei pomodori in scatolaWhat are the benefits of canned tomatoes?
pages.title: Le lattine possono essere riciclate all'infinito, ma questo processo richiede molta energiaCans can be recycled endlessly but this process costs a lot of energy ⚡️
Ma i pomodori in scatola hanno anche altri vantaggi But there are some other benefits to canned tomatoes 🥫
Quali sono i vantaggi dei pomodori in scatolaWhat is a benefit of canned tomatoes?

answerOptions.title: Prezzo migliore per gli agricoltoriBetter price for farmers💰
Meno pomodori sprecati Fewer wasted tomatoes 🗑

answerExplanation.text:
I pomodori in scatola hanno un minor spreco perché, rispetto ai pomodori freschi, si spreca meno nelle nostre cucine. In entrambi i casi, si verifica un notevole spreco nella fase di coltivazione. In totale, il 54% dei pomodori freschi viene sprecato, e il 41% dei pomodori in scatola.

Un altro vantaggio dei pomodori in scatola è che vengono lavorati subito dopo la raccolta, quindi conservano più nutrienti rispetto ai pomodori freschi trasportatiCanned tomatoes have a lower waste because compared to fresh tomatoes there is less waste in our own kitchens. In both cases, there is quite some waste that happens at the farming stage. In total, 54% of fresh tomatoes are wasted, and 41% of canned tomatoes.

Another benefit of canned tomatoes is that they are processed right after harvest, so they retain more nutrients than transported fresh tomatoes.

dailyContent.notificationText: Come i legumi migliorano il terrenoHow legumes improve the soil 🫘 - Recipe of the day: Curry di patate dolci e lenticchieSweet Potato and Lentil Curry

home.title: Come i legumi migliorano il terrenoHow legumes improve the soil

pages.title: Ormai avrete capito che i legumi sono ottimi per il clima 🫘 Scopriamo insieme come funzionaBy now you’ve seen that legumes are great for the climate 🫘 Let’s find out how exactly that works.
Cosa rende speciali le radici dei legumiWhat makes the roots of legumes special?

answerOptions.title: Permettono al terreno di immagazzinare più acquaThey allow the soil to store more water 💦
Aggiungono azoto al terreno They add nitrogen to the soil ⚡️
Attraggono i vermi che migliorano il terreno They attract worms that improve the soil 🪱

answerExplanation.text:
L'azoto è un ingrediente chiave per le piante. Invece di limitarsi ad utilizzarlo, le leguminose lo aggiungono anche al terreno. Ciò avviene attraverso una 'collaborazione' con i batteri che vivono intorno alle loro radici.

Questo significa che gli agricoltori possono utilizzare le leguminose al posto dei fertilizzanti azotati, con conseguente riduzione delle emissioni e molti altri vantaggiNitrogen is a key ingredient for plants. Instead of only using nitrogen, legumes also ADD it to the soil. This happens through a ‘partnership’ with bacteria that like to live around their roots.

This means that farmers can use legumes instead of nitrogen fertilizer, which saves emissions and has lots of other benefits.

pages.subtitle: Un delizioso curry con lenticchie per migliorare il terrenoA delicious curry with lentils to improve the soil!

pages.hometitle: Come possiamo aumentare ulteriormente il potenziale dei legumiHow can we increase the potential of legumes even more?
pages.title: Quando un agricoltore usa i fagioli nel campo, ha bisogno di meno fertilizzante per coltivare il granoWhen a farmer uses beans in the field, he needs less fertilizer to grow wheat 🫘
Esiste un metodo di coltivazione speciale che potenzia ulteriormente le straordinarie proprietà dei legumi There is a special farming method that boosts the special power of legumes even more 🧑🏼‍🌾
Come possiamo aumentare il potenziale dei legumiHow can we increase the potential of legumes? 🫘
pages.subtitle: per fissare l'azoto nel terrenoto fix nitrogen in the soil

answerOptions.title: Coltivare i fagioli prima di coltivare il granoGrowing beans before growing wheat
Coltivare fagioli e grano insieme in un unico campoGrowing beans and wheat together in one field

answerExplanation.text:
Questo metodo di coltivare diverse piante nello stesso campo contemporaneamente si chiama consociazione. Aumenta la resa delle leguminose, consente di risparmiare terreno ed è anche più bello di una monocoltura.

L'utilizzo della consociazione per tutte le leguminose potrebbe ridurre del 26% il fabbisogno di fertilizzanti chimici. Questo è un grande vantaggio perché (come avete scoperto qualche articolo fa) i fertilizzanti hanno un forte impatto sul climaThis method of growing different plants in one field at the same time is called intercropping. It boosts the power of legumes, saves land, and also looks more beautiful than a monoculture.

Using intercropping for all legumes could reduce the need for chemical fertilizer by 26%. This is a big deal because (as you discovered a few stories back) fertilizer has a big climate impact!

dailyContent.notificationText: Ci sono milioni di maiali nei Paesi Bassi. Dove sono?There are millions of pigs in the Netherlands. Where are they? 🐷 - Recipe of the day: Vegetable Jambalaya

home.title: Hidden pigs

pages.title: The Netherlands uses a star system to show the wellbeing of animals. A meat product can have 0-3 stars ⭐️
Only the pigs with 2 or 3 stars live in a stable where they can go outside 🐷
How many of the pigs in the Netherlands never come outside?

answerExplanation.text:
Purtroppo è molto difficile trovare carne di maiale a 2-3 stelle. Il benessere degli animali suini è un problema serio.

Vediamo spesso mucche nei campi, mentre i maiali sono nascosti alla vista. Non li vedi mai, anche se nell’UE ci sono molti più maiali che muccheUnfortunately it is very difficult to find 2-3 star pork. The animal welfare of pigs is a serious issue.

We often see cows in the fields, while pigs are hidden out of sight. You never see them, even though there are way more pigs than cows in the Netherlands and the EU.

pages.subtitle: La paprika affumicata in polvere è un ottimo modo per sostituire il sapore del bacon o di altra carne.Smoked paprika powder is an excellent way to replace the taste of bacon of other meat.

pages.hometitle: QuantiHow many of the pollichickens vedno la luce del giorno see daylight in the Netherlands?
pages.title: How many of the broiler pollichickens for Dutch supermarkets see daylight? 🐓
pages.subtitle: broiler pollichickens are raised for meat

answerExplanation.text:
Dal 2023, tutta la carne di pollo nei supermercati olandesi ha almeno 1 stella. E i polli con una stella possono uscire all'aperto (a differenza dei maiali con 1 stella). Sono inoltre di una razza che cresce più lentamente.

C'è ancora molto lavoro da fare, per esempio, il pollo a 0 stelle che cresce più velocemente di quanto sia sano per il pollo stesso esiste ancora nei Paesi Bassi per l'esportazione. Ma questo successo dimostra che le campagne per il benessere animale funzionano!

Tutto il merito va a Wakker Dier, che ha iniziato la campagna nel 2013 (!). Leggi la loro storia nel link.Since 2023, all chicken meat in Dutch supermarkets has at least 1 star. And pollichickens with a 1-star rating can go outside (unlike 1-star pigs). They are also a breed that grows slower.

A lot of work still needs to be done, for example, 0-star chicken that grows faster than is healthy for the chicken still exists in the Netherlands for export. But this success shows that campaigns for animal welfare work!

All the credit goes to Wakker Dier which started campaigning in 2013 (!). Read their story in the link.

dailyContent.notificationText: What is organic farming? 👩‍🌾 - Recipe of the day: Red Cabbage Stir-fry

home.title: Che cos'è l'agricoltura biologica?What is organic farming?

pages.title: Il cibo biologico non è solo un'etichetta ma anche una mentalità.Organic food is not just a label but also a mindset. 🤓
Per comprendere meglio l'agricoltura biologica, dobbiamo confrontarla con l'agricoltura industriale.To better understand organic farming, we need to compare it with industrial agriculture.
Che cos'è l'agricoltura industriale?What is industrial agriculture?

answerOptions.title: Usare la tecnologia invece degli animaliUsing technology instead of animals 🚜
Usare sostanze chimiche per produrre la maggiore quantità possibile di ciboUsing chemicals to grow the most food possible 🧪
Creare aziende agricole più grandi per risparmiare denaroCreating bigger farms to save money 💰

answerExplanation.text:
L'agricoltura industriale punta tutto sulla resa: ottenere più cibo dalla terra con ogni mezzo necessario. Questo ha reso la produzione alimentare molto efficiente, ma sta anche distruggendo il suolo da cui dipendiamo.

L'agricoltura biologica mira a mantenere un equilibrio tra suolo sano e produzione di cibo. Ecco perché utilizza rotazioni delle colture, letame e compost.Industrial agriculture is all about output: getting more food from the land, by any means necessary. This made food production very efficient but it’s also destroying the soil we depend on.

Organic agriculture is about keeping a balance between healthy soil and food production. That’s why it uses crop rotations, manure, and compost.

pages.subtitle: Una ricetta con le mele, uno dei prodotti di cui è facile comprare la versione biologica.A recipe with apples, one of the products it’s easy to buy the organic version.

pages.hometitle: Quanto è diffusa l'agricoltura biologica nell'UE?How big is organic farming in the EU?
pages.title: Il 10% di tutti i terreni agricoli nell'UE è biologico10% of all farmland in the EU is organic 👨🏻‍🌾
Qual è l'obiettivo dell'UE per il 2030?What is the EU goal for 2030? 🇪🇺
pages.subtitle: per la quota di agricoltura biologicafor the share of organic agriculture

answerOptions.title: Non c'è un obiettivoThere is no goal

answerExplanation.text:
La buona notizia è che l'agricoltura biologica è in aumento nell'UE e negli Stati Uniti.

La cattiva notizia: al ritmo attuale, l'UE non raggiungerà il suo obiettivo. L'Austria è l'unico paese che lo ha già raggiunto. Negli Stati Uniti, l'agricoltura biologica rappresenta meno dell'1% di tutti i terreni agricoli.

Acquistare più prodotti biologici aiuta la transizione. Inizia con uno o due prodotti che acquisti sempre in versione biologica. Leggi di più sul nostro punto di vista nel nostro blog.The good news is that organic agriculture is on the rise in the EU and the US.

The bad news: at the current rate the EU won’t reach its target. Austria is the only country that already reached it. In the US, organic agriculture is less than 1% of all farmland.

Buying more organic products helps the transition. Start with one or two products that you always buy organically. Read more about our take on our blog.

dailyContent.notificationText: Che aspetto ha il caffè sostenibile?What does sustainable coffee look like? ☕️ - Recipe of the day: Tagliatelle ai funghi con vino biancoWhite Wine Mushroom Tagliatelle

home.title: Che aspetto ha il caffè sostenibile?What does sustainable coffee look like?

pages.title: Le piante di caffè e cacao originariamente crescono all'ombra della forestaCoffee and cacao plants and trees originally grow in the shade of the forest 🌳
Ma per aumentare la produzione, molte piantagioni ora coltivano caffè o cacao direttamente al soleBut to increase production, many farms now grow coffee or cacao directly in the sun ☀️

Questo significa che la foresta viene abbattuta per fare spazio alle piantagioniThat means forest is cut down to make space for the plantations 🪓 Una delle soluzioni è l'‘agroforestazione’.One of the solutions is ‘agroforestry’.
Che aspetto ha il caffè sostenibile?What does sustainable coffee look like?

answerOptions.title: Trasformare i terreni agricoli in foresteTurning farmland into forests
Coltivare cibo tra gli alberiGrowing food amongst trees
Tagliare gli alberi in modo sostenibileCutting trees in a sustainable way

answerExplanation.text:
L'agroforestazione è la combinazione della produzione alimentare con la foresta. Questo rende possibile trasformare le piantagioni di caffè in foreste. Anche la qualità migliora grazie a una maggiore ombreggiatura.

Il modo migliore per trovare questi prodotti è cercare caffè o cioccolato di alta qualità e monorigine. È più trasparente riguardo alla loro provenienza e spesso più sostenibile ed equo.Agroforestry is mixing food production with forest. That makes it possible to turn coffee plantations into forests. The quality also improves because there is more shade.

The best way to find these products is to look for high-quality, single-origin coffee or chocolate. It’s more transparent where it comes from and often more sustainable and fair.

pages.subtitle: Questa ricetta include le noci. E poiché queste provengono dagli alberi, possono anch'esse provenire da sistemi agroforestali.This recipe includes walnuts. And since those come from trees, they can also come from agroforestry systems.

pages.title: L'esempio del caffè è noto come 'agroforestazione multistrato'The coffee example is known as ‘multistrata agroforestry’ 🌳 È un'importante soluzione climatica nel Project DrawdownIt’s an important climate solution in Project Drawdown
Esistono anche altre forme di agroforestazione che possono risparmiare grandi quantità di CO2There are also other forms of agroforestry that can save lots of CO2 ⬇️
Quale tipo di agroforestazione è più comune in Europa?What type of agroforestry is most common in Europe?

answerOptions.title: Combinare alberi con animali al pascoloCombining trees with grazing animals 🐄
Combinare alberi da frutto con verdureCombining fruit trees with vegetables 🥦
Combinare produzione alimentare e produzione di legnoCombining food and wood production 🪵

answerExplanation.text:
'Silvopastorale' è un metodo agricolo antico in cui gli animali pascolano tra gli alberi. La forma più comune è la dehesa spagnola, dove gli animali vivono tra le querce. È anche possibile combinare animali o persino la produzione di verdure con noci, alberi da frutto o ulivi.

Se il silvopastorale sostituisce i prati intensivi, è un'altra importante soluzione climatica con molti benefici: suoli più sani, ombra per gli animali e paesaggi più belli!‘Silvopasture’ is an ancient farming method where animals graze between trees. The most common form is the Spanish dehesa, where animals live between oak trees. It’s also possible to combine animals or even vegetable production with nut, fruit, or olive trees.

If silvopasture replaces intensive grasslands it is another important climate solution with lots of benefits: healthier soils, shade for the animals, and more beautiful landscapes!

dailyContent.notificationText: Ecco alcuni modi semplici per aggiungere proteine extraHere are some easy ways to add extra protein 💪 - Recipe of the day: Purè di sedano rapa e patate dolciCeleriac Sweet Potato Mash

home.title: Il modo più semplice per aggiungere più proteineThe easiest way to add more protein

pages.title: Noci e legumi sono un'ottima fonte di proteineNuts and legumes are a great source of protein 🫘🥜
Specialmente perché puoi aggiungerli facilmente a qualsiasi pasto.Especially because you can easily add them to any meal.
Quale alimento ha più proteine?Which food has the most protein? 💪
pages.subtitle: per 100 g

answerExplanation.text:
Abbiamo già scoperto che la maggior parte delle persone si preoccupa troppo delle proteine. Ma se segui un'alimentazione completamente vegetale, è utile sapere quali alimenti aggiungono proteine al tuo pasto.

Una manciata di semi di zucca o di girasole è un ottimo modo per aggiungere proteine extra. Danno anche una piacevole croccantezza a qualsiasi pasto, dallo yogurt al riso e alla pasta.We already discovered that most people worry too much about protein. But if you eat completely plant-based, it is useful to know which foods add protein to your meal.

A hand full of pumpkin or sunflower seeds is a great way to add some extra protein. They also provide a tasty crunch to any meal, from yogurt to rice and pasta.

pages.subtitle: I semi di zucca in questa ricetta forniscono proteine extra e croccantezza.The pumpkin seeds in this recipe provide some extra protein and crunch.

pages.hometitle: Le noci contengono molti grassi. Quanto è salutare?Nuts contain a lot of fat. How healthy is that?
pages.title: Le noci possono avere molte proteine, ma hanno anche molti grassiNuts might have a lot of protein but they also have a lot of fat 🥜
I grassi si presentano in due forme: saturi (cattivi) e insaturi (buoni)Fats come in two forms: saturated (bad) and unsaturated (good) 🤓
Cosa contiene la minore quantità di grassi saturi (cattivi)?What contains the least amount of saturated (bad) fat?

answerOptions.title: Tre cucchiai di burro di arachidiThree spoons of peanut butter (50g) 🥜

answerExplanation.text:
Una porzione tipica di noci contiene meno grassi saturi di una porzione tipica di carne o latticini.

Le noci contengono molti grassi, ma questi sono per lo più insaturi. Ciò significa che le noci hanno molti benefici per la salute. Riducono il colesterolo e il rischio di diabete di tipo 2 e malattie cardiache. Gli studi inoltre non hanno trovato una connessione con l'aumento di peso. E naturalmente sono deliziose 🤤

Il rapporto EAT Lancet raccomanda di mangiarne 50 g al giorno!A typical portion size of nuts has less saturated fat than a typical serving of meat or dairy.

Nuts contain a lot of fat but these are mostly unsaturated. This means that nuts have many health benefits. They reduce cholesterol and the risk of type 2 diabetes and heart disease. Studies also didn’t find a connection to weight gain. And of course they’re delicious 🤤

The EAT Lancet report recommends 50 g a day!

dailyContent.notificationText: La storia del pesce d'allevamento 🐟 - Ricetta del giorno: Zuppa di noodle con funghi e zenzeroThe story of farmed fish 🐟 - Recipe of the day: Mushroom Ginger Noodle Soup

home.title: La storia del pesce d'allevamentoThe story of farmed fish

pages.title: Prima che gli umani iniziassero a coltivare, cacciavano animali selvatici per la carne 🏹. Ciò causò l'estinzione di molti grandi mammiferi come il mammut.Before humans started farming, we hunted wild animals for meat 🏹. This caused the extinction of many big mammals like the mammoth.

La pesca è come la caccia 🎣. E per questo motivo, molti pesci sono in pericolo. Come soluzione - allo stesso modo che abbiamo fatto con gli animali sulla terraferma - abbiamo avviato allevamenti ittici.Fishing is like hunting 🎣. And because of that, many fish are endangered. As a solution - like we did with animals on the land - we started fish farms.
Cosa hanno in comune il pesce d'allevamento e la carne d'allevamento?What do farmed fish and farmed meat have in common? 🐟🐖

answerOptions.title: Mangiano solo piante (erbivori)They only eat plants (herbivores)
Richiede terra per coltivare il loro mangimeIt requires land to grow their feed

answerExplanation.text:
Il pesce d'allevamento causa gli stessi problemi della carne d'allevamento: serve molta terra per nutrirlo. A volte gli allevamenti pescano addirittura pesci selvatici da usare come mangime. Nel complesso, è lo stesso problema in acqua come sulla terraferma: vogliamo mangiare prodotti che richiedono molto mangime.

Invece, dobbiamo mangiare cibi a minore impatto. Noci e legumi dalla terra. Cozze, pesci più piccoli come l'aringa e alghe dall'oceano.Farmed fish causes the same problems as farmed meat: it takes lots of land to feed them. Sometimes farms even catch wild fish to use as feed. Overall it’s the same problem in water as on land: we want to eat products that require a lot of feed.

Instead, we need to eat lower-impact foods. Nuts and legumes from the land. Mussels, smaller fish like herring, and seaweed from the ocean.

pages.subtitle: I pesci d'allevamento vengono nutriti con soia. Meglio mangiarla noi stessi, come in questa ricetta, dove usiamo il tofu.Farmed fish are fed with soy. Better to eat it ourselves, like in this recipe, where we use tofu.

pages.hometitle: La maggior parte del nostro pesce è selvatico o allevato?Is most of our fish wild or farmed?
pages.title: Cosa fornisce più prodotti ittici?What provides more seafood? 🍣
pages.subtitle: per gli umanifor humans

answerExplanation.text:
Il 53% dei prodotti ittici mondiali proviene da allevamenti ittici. Nel 1960 era solo il 6%. Per questo motivo, oggi viene usata più soia per nutrire i pesci che per produrre tofu!

Catturare pesci selvatici e allevare pesci crea diversi tipi di problemi. Ciò che vale per entrambi i contesti è che dobbiamo passare a prodotti ittici con un'impronta ecologica minore: pesci piccoli dalla pesca selvatica e cozze e alghe dagli allevamenti.53% of the world’s seafood comes from fish farms. In 1960 it was only 6%. Because of this, more soy is now used to feed fish than to create tofu!

Catching fish in the wild and farming fish creates different kinds of issues. What applies to both contexts is that we need to switch to seafood with a lower footprint: small fish from the wild and mussels and seaweed from farms.

dailyContent.notificationText: L'impatto del cibo biologico 🌱 - Ricetta del giorno: CaponataThe impact of organic food 🌱 - Recipe of the day: Caponata

home.title: L'impatto del cibo biologicoThe impact of organic food

pages.title: Uno studio francese ha mostrato che i prodotti biologici riducono la tua impronta di carbonio. Ma c'è un trucco.A French study showed that organic products decrease your carbon footprint. But there is a twist.
Il cibo biologico riduce la tua impronta solo se anche…Organic food only reduces your footprint if you also…

answerOptions.title: ...lo acquisti nei piccoli negozi di fattoria...buy it at small farm shops 👩‍🌾
...mangi meno carne e latticini...eat less meat and dairy 🥩

answerExplanation.text:
L'agricoltura biologica ha molti benefici ma c'è un aspetto negativo: richiede un po' più di terra. Quindi se vogliamo continuare a mangiare la stessa quantità di carne, dovremmo usare ancora più terra per coltivare il mangime per animali.

La soluzione è mangiare meno carne e latticini. Questo libera molta terra agricola che possiamo poi utilizzare per l'agricoltura biologica.Organic farming has lots of benefits but there is one downside: it requires a bit more land. So if we want to keep eating the same amount of meat, we would have to use even more land to grow the animal feed.

The solution is eating less meat and dairy. This frees up lots of farmland we can then use for organic agriculture.

pages.subtitle: I prodotti a base di grano richiedono solitamente molto fertilizzante chimico, quindi comprare pasta biologica è un'ottima scelta.Wheat products usually require a lot of chemical fertilizer, so buying organic pasta is a great choice.

pages.hometitle: La carne biologica è migliore per il clima?Is organic meat better for the climate?
pages.title: L'impronta di carbonio della carne biologica è...The carbon footprint of organic meat is… 🥩
pages.subtitle: in media, rispetto alla carne convenzionaleon average, compared to conventional meat

answerOptions.title: Più o meno la stessaAbout the same 🟰

answerExplanation.text:
La carne delle fattorie biologiche può avere benefici, ad esempio, è generalmente molto migliore per gli animali. Ma una cosa è chiara nei dati: non ha necessariamente un'impronta di carbonio inferiore.

Ancora una volta possiamo concludere che COSA mangi è molto più importante di COME viene prodotto.Meat from organic farms might have benefits, for example, it’s usually much better for the animals. But one thing is clear in the data: it doesn’t necessarily have a lower carbon footprint.

Once again we can conclude that WHAT you eat is much more important than HOW it is produced.

dailyContent.notificationTitle: Today: la Prateria VerdeMoo Moo Meadows
dailyContent.notificationText: Qual è l'impatto dell'uso della panna di soia? 🐄 - Ricetta del giorno: Pasta cremosa alle pereWhat’s the impact of using soy cream? 🐄 - Recipe of the day: Creamy Pear Pasta

home.title: Qual è l'impatto dell'uso della panna di soia?What’s the impact of using soy cream?

pages.title: Forse avrai notato che menzioniamo 'panna (di soia)' nelle nostre ricette.Maybe you’ve noticed that we mention ‘(soy)cream’ in our recipes.
Significa che lasciamo la scelta a te: usare panna di soia o panna caseariaIt means that we leave the choice up to you: use soy cream or dairy cream 🐄

Ma abbiamo iniziato a chiederci: qual è la differenza? Quindi abbiamo calcolato l'impatto per la nostra 'Pasta cremosa alle pere'But we started wondering: what’s the difference? So we calculated the impact for our ‘Creamy Pear Pasta’ 🍝
Sostituire la panna casearia con la panna di soia risparmia le stesse emissioni di CO2 di...Replacing dairy cream with soy cream saves the same CO2 emissions as…

answerOptions.title: Togliere la pastaLeaving out the pasta 🍝 (520 g CO2)
Togliere le pereLeaving out the pears 🍐 (280 g CO2)

answerExplanation.text:
Cucinare con 250 ml di panna di soia invece di quella casearia annulla l'impronta di 400 grammi di pasta! È più di quanto pensassimo.

Se non la trovi nel tuo negozio locale, non sentirti troppo in colpa. Ma per chi vuole fare del suo meglio, cucinare con panna vegetale è un passo che vale la pena fare.Cooking with 250 ml soy cream instead of dairy cream cancels out the footprint of 400 grams of pasta! That is more than we thought.

If you can’t find it in your local store, don’t feel too bad. But for anyone who wants to do their best, cooking with plant-based cream is a step worth taking.

pages.subtitle: Puoi anche usare altra panna da cucina vegetale, ma la panna di soia è quella che preferiamo di più.You can also use other plant-based cooking cream, but we like soy cream the most.

pages.hometitle: Quanto è salutare la panna di soia?How healthy is soy cream?
pages.title: Abbiamo scoperto recentemente che la panna di soia è fatta con olio di girasole… 🌻 Questo la rende malsana?We recently found out that soy cream is made with sunflower oil… 🌻 Does that make it unhealthy?
Quale tipo di panna contiene meno grassi saturi?Which type of cream contains less saturated fat?

answerExplanation.text:
La panna serve ad aggiungere grasso, sapore e cremosità. Di solito non è il prodotto più salutare.

Ma la panna di soia, anche se è fatta con olio di girasole, è un'opzione più salutare della maggior parte delle panne casearie. Ha una quantità MOLTO inferiore di grassi saturi (2 g rispetto a 12-19 g). Ha anche meno calorie.Cream is about adding fat, taste, and creaminess. It’s usually not the healthiest product.

But soy cream, even though it’s made with sunflower oil, is a healthier option than most dairy cream. It has a MUCH lower amount of saturated fat (2g compared to 12-19g). It also has fewer calories.

dailyContent.notificationText: What Europe needs to do about palm oil 🌴 - Recipe of the day: Indonesian Spicy Cabbage

home.title: What Europe needs to do about palm oil

pages.title: As we discovered in the ‘Fading Forest’, palm oil is a big cause of deforestation 🪓
While most palm oil is used as cooking oil in Asia, Europe also imports 20% of the world’s palm oil 🌴
Where is most palm oil used in the EU?

answerExplanation.text:
The majority of palm oil in the EU is used as biofuel. The good news is that it will be phased out by 2030. Still, the question is what it will be replaced with.

Here we have to draw a conclusion that’s not about food: ride your bike or take public transport more than a car!

pages.subtitle: A tribute to Indonesia, where most palm oil comes from.

pages.hometitle: Is there a sustainable form of biofuel?
pages.title: Cutting down rainforests to grow palm oil as fuel for cars or airplanes is a terrible idea 🪓
But there is also a sustainable form of biofuel: used cooking oil from frying food 🍟
What is the biggest issue with used cooking oil as fuel? ✈️

answerOptions.title: It creates less CO2 but more air pollution
It makes the engine less efficient

answerExplanation.text:
Flying from London to New York requires about 1 bathtub of fuel per passenger! If you use all the cooking oil from every McDonald's in London for a whole year, it doesn’t even cover 0,1% of the yearly flights from London to New York.

There is nowhere near enough used cooking oil. Unfortunately, sustainable flying just isn’t possible. Maybe we can have electric airplanes in the future, but that is very very far away…

dailyContent.notificationText: What you should know about water pollution ☠️ - Recipe of the day: Thai Peanut Curry

home.title: Dead zones in the water

pages.title: Today we’re going to look at ‘eutrophication’. It’s a process where rivers, lakes and the ocean become polluted 🌊
It starts when water has too many nutrients. This causes the growth of algae at the surface and blocks sunlight 🦠
In the end, plants and fish suffocate and die ☠️
Where do the polluting nutrients come from?

answerExplanation.text:
In Europe and the US, most of the pollution comes from agriculture. In other regions it also comes from wastewater.

The products with the highest pollution are meat and dairy. That’s because their feed requires so much fertilizer. And because of the high-energy feed, their poop is then also full of extra nutrients and causes pollution.

pages.subtitle: The best way to reduce water pollution is eating more plant-based. Curries are always a great option!

pages.hometitle: What happened to the world’s biggest dead zone?
pages.title: One of the biggest dead zones in the world was created in the Black Sea in the 1990s ☠️
What happened since then? 💧

answerExplanation.text:
One report identified 415 areas with eutrophication problems (and many regions don’t even have enough data). Of all those areas, only 13 are in recovery. Today the biggest dead zone is in the EU: the Baltic Sea.

But it’s hopeful that the recovery of a dead zone is possible within 15-20 years if we take the right actions.

dailyContent.notificationText: The unfair price of rice 🍚 - Recipe of the day: Fennel Risotto

home.title: The unfair price of rice

pages.title: Many of the products we enjoy in Europe come from tropical countries. Often, those farmers and workers don’t get paid enough 👨🏾‍🌾
For example, a rice farmer in Thailand only gets 56% of what they need to make a decent living 💸
The average price for white rice in the Netherlands is € 1.69 per kg 🍚 If the farmer gets paid a fair amount, how much would it increase the price?
How much would the price increase? 🍚
pages.subtitle: to provide a decent living standard, assuming that rice costs € 1.69 per kg

answerExplanation.text:
Farmers and workers of many products get significanlty underpaid. But it wouldn’t actually cost much to fix this problem.

To better understand why this happens, we need to explore where most of the money ends up. That’s for the next story in Fair Fields.

pages.subtitle: Arborio rice for risotto doesn’t come from Thailand, but from Italy.

pages.hometitle: What exactly is the purpose of ‘Fairtrade’?
pages.title: The goal of the Fairtrade label is to tackle unfair price differences 💰 How does it work?
Fairtrade determines…

answerExplanation.text:
Fairtrade only ensures that farmers get paid a fair price. It’s up to retailers to determine the price that consumers pay for a product. So Fairtrade is not automatically a good deal for consumers, but it is a better deal for farmers. We’ll explore how exactly Fairtrade works for farmers in a later story.

And how does the Fairtrade organization make money? Products that use the Fairtrade logo pay a licensing fee. For rice in the Netherlands, this is 0,09€ per kg.

dailyContent.notificationText: Which food would not exist without pollinators? 🌼 - Recipe of the day: Beetroot and Pumpkin Curry

home.title: The pollinator team

pages.title: Many plants rely on insects to spread their pollen and reproduce 🌼
Bees are the most famous pollinators but lots of other insects like butterflies and flies are also part of the team 🦋
Which food would not exist without pollinators?

answerExplanation.text:
The good news is that the foods we eat most of - rice, wheat, and potatoes - are not dependent on pollinators. But a lot of the foods that make life better are dependent: from apples to chocolate.

That is one of the many reasons why it’s important to care for the pollinator team.

pages.subtitle: Pumpkin is another product that wouldn’t exist without pollinators.

pages.hometitle: What is the most effective way to help pollinators in your garden?
pages.title: What can you do yourself to increase pollinators in your own backyard? 🦋
The results from lots of studies are mixed 🧐 But there are few things that always help.
What helps pollinators the most? 🌼

answerExplanation.text:
A higher diversity of plant species always increased pollinators. The results were not as clear for native vs exotic plants.

Three other things consistently showed positive effects: increasing the size of the garden, having more shrubs and trees, and more exposure to the sun.

dailyContent.notificationText: The only downside of nuts 🥜 - Recipe of the day: Garlic Lime Spaghetti

home.title: The only downside of nuts

pages.title: We’ve seen that nuts are full of protein and have a much lower footprint than meat 🥜
But when it comes to water use, they’re in the same category as beef… 💧
Which nuts have the lowest water footprint?

answerExplanation.text:
Peanuts and walnuts use the least water. But it’s true that especially almonds, cashews, and pistachios are very thirsty plants. This is a problem in dry areas.

At the same time, nuts score MUCH better than meat on all other environmental issues. That means they are still a good source of protein in the transition to a more sustainable food system.

pages.subtitle: Peanuts have the lowest water footprint of nuts. They usually work well in rice recipes, but here they are a great fit for this pasta.

pages.hometitle: What’s the most sustainable nut if we look at all factors?
pages.title: One study wanted to find out: how can we buy responsible nuts? 🥜
They scored nuts and seeds on sustainability, but also on nutritional value and social aspects like worker safety 👷🏽
Which factor is most important for the sustainability of nuts?

answerOptions.title: Amount of protein in the nut 💪

answerExplanation.text:
The biggest influence on all factors was the type of nut. They divided them into three categories and walnuts and sunflower seeds scored the best in all aspects. We quickly analyzed our own recipes after learning this and good news: we use walnuts most often!

Peanuts and hazelnuts belong to the second category. Cashews, almonds, and chestnuts have the most issues. We have quite some recipes with cashews so we’ll pay attention to this when making new recipes.

Replacing meat with nuts is an important part of a sustainable diet and we don’t think you should feel guilty about eating cashews or almonds. But let’s focus on eating more walnuts.

dailyContent.notificationText: The footprint of home delivery 🏠 - Recipe of the day: Creamy Cabbage Potato Mash

home.title: The footprint of home delivery

pages.title: Imagine you’ve found a really nice farm shop in your area that sells organic vegetables 👩‍🌾
You can buy their veggie box at the shop but they can also send it to your house 📦
If the farm shop is 4 km away, which option has a lower footprint?
pages.subtitle: assuming both drive on petrol

answerExplanation.text:
As we discovered before, food miles have a much lower footprint than people think. But there is one case where every mile matters: when you’re driving yourself! Because that’s a lot of emissions and traffic for only one delivery, compared to a truck full of veggie boxes.

At Fork Ranger, we don’t believe that everything should be delivered 24/7. More convenience is not the solution to a sustainable world. But if the alternative is driving a car, delivery is usually the better option.

pages.subtitle: Even though food miles are not a useful measure, buying local food is still important for other reasons. This recipe uses lots of ingredients from the Netherlands.

pages.hometitle: Eating locally: what is the biggest benefit for farmers?
pages.title: We found a study that summarizes a lot of research about local food systems, like farmers’ markets 👩🏽‍🌾
What is the main reason for farmers to participate in local food initiatives?

answerExplanation.text:
Often social recognition and contact with consumers was the main reason for farmers to get involved in local food initiatives.

Another interesting finding was that consumers are healthier when they buy food locally. Not because local food is healthier, but probably because they are more aware of what they eat.

Overall you might say that the main benefit of local food is being more mindful about our food, feeling connected to where it comes from, and appreciating who grew it.

dailyContent.notificationText: How to get enough vitamin B12 ✨ - Recipe of the day: Carrot Cauliflower Biryani

home.title: How to get enough vitamin B12

pages.title: There is one nutrient that’s difficult to get with only plants: vitamin B12 ✨
The EAT-Lancet report calculated what a healthy and sustainable diet should look like. Overall, it’s about eating lots of veggies, legumes, and nuts 🥦🥜
But meat and dairy also play a role and provide the recommended amount of B12 🥛
What is the recommended amount of dairy per day? 🥛
pages.subtitle: according to the EAT-Lancet report

answerExplanation.text:
If you eat according to the EAT Lancet diet, you should get enough vitamin B12. Many plant-based meats and dairy alternatives also contain B12.

If you eat less meat & dairy than this, it’s important to pay attention and consider a Vitamin B12 supplement.

pages.subtitle: Of all dairy products, yogurt has the lowest footprint. And in this recipe it’s a delicious addition!

pages.hometitle: Where does vitamin B12 come from?
pages.title: Let’s take a closer look at where vitamin B12 comes from ✨
Vitamin B12 is created by…

answerExplanation.text:
B12 can only be made by microbes like bacteria. Sometimes plants or fungi contain B12 because they work together with bacteria, but the amounts are very low. The meat from many animals (pollichickens, pigs, fish) contains B12 because they eat other things that contain B12.

The microbes that create B12 also live inside the stomachs of ruminants like cows, which is why their meat and dairy contain B12.

dailyContent.notificationText: The plastic dilemma, can you do something about it? 🥤 - Recipe of the day: Fennel Orange Couscous

home.title: The plastic dilemma

pages.title: We already covered that packaging is usually a small part of the climate impact of food 🥫 But if you want to do something about it, what can you do?
We were really frustrated researching this… the packaging industry is messed up! 🫙
For example, we all know about plastic pollution, but plastic also has benefits 🥤
What is the benefit of plastic packaging?

answerExplanation.text:
It’s confusing and frustrating to solve the packaging dilemmas as a consumer. The future solution is saying goodbye to single-use packaging: reusing bottles and taking your bags and boxes with you. But living this way is almost impossible in our current world.

So our advice is to avoid packaging where you can. Not to become 100% zero waste, but as a way of unlearning the convenience that single-use packaging brings. Start with bread: we buy it regularly and in many supermarkets it’s possible to put it in a reusable bag.

pages.subtitle: This is a recipe with little packaging because fennel, oranges, and zucchini are usually not wrapped in plastic.

pages.hometitle: Is plastic pollution in Europe more under control than in China?
pages.title: Most of the plastic waste that enters the Ocean comes from China 🇨🇳
China’s lost plastic waste is almost 10 times higher than in Europe 🇪🇺 But what about microplastics?
Compared to China, how much microplastics enter the Ocean from Europe?

answerExplanation.text:
In Europe and the US, we have pretty advanced waste treatment, which prevents most of the plastic pollution from entering the environment. But our microplastic pollution is on the same level as China.

When we talk about plastic pollution we often think of packaging, but in Europe, almost half the pollution comes from microplastics from other sources like washing synthetic clothing and wear and tear of car tires.

dailyContent.notificationText: Which food provides the most Vitamin B12? 💊 - Recipe of the day: Nasi Goreng

home.title: Which food provides the most Vitamin B12?

pages.title: B12 is a vitamin you can only get from animal foods 🐐 But not all animal foods contain the same amount…
Which food provides the most Vitamin B12?
pages.subtitle: for the same weight

answerExplanation.text:
Salmon contains by far the most B12, and it also has a lower footprint than dairy and other meat. That makes it the most climate-friendly way to fill up your B12 levels.

Eggs and dairy also have a relatively high amount of B12 for a low footprint. Beef has even more B12 but also a much higher footprint. Pork contains very little B12.

Good to know: often plant-based meat and dairy alternatives also contain B12.

pages.subtitle: Serve this Nasi Goreng with an egg for a good addition of vitamin B12.

pages.hometitle: How much vitamin B12 is in meat alternatives?
pages.title: A Dutch report compared the nutritional value of meat alternatives to meat 🍔
Often meat alternatives are enriched with vitamins and minerals like iron ✨
How many of the meat replacements were enriched with vitamin B12?

answerExplanation.text:
Those 55% that were enriched had relatively high levels of B12. But since not all products are enriched, it can be tricky to get enough B12 when you only rely on meat alternatives.

ProVeg - the NGO that did the research - advises to take B12 supplements when eating (mostly) plant-based.

dailyContent.notificationText: How animal feed can be sustainable 🐷 - Recipe of the day: Easy Pesto Lasagne

home.title: How animal feed can be sustainable

pages.title: The main problem with animals in the food system at the moment is that we use valuable land to grow food for them 🌽
But there are also ways that meat can be sustainable. That’s what we’re going to explore in The Good Ranch.
How can animals be part of a sustainable food system? 🐷
pages.subtitle: They...

answerOptions.title: ... eat plants that are toxic for humans

answerExplanation.text:
There are many by-products from food processing. For example, when making sunflower oil you are left with a dry ‘cake’ that we can’t eat but is nutritious feed for animals.

Another important role that especially pigs can play is eating food waste (although it’s always more effective to first eat leftover food ourselves).

pages.subtitle: Choose whole grain lasagne: there are a lot of by-products from milling white flour that are fed to animals. But it’s a lot more sustainable to eat the entire grain of wheat ourselves.

pages.hometitle: What makes up the majority of byproducts that are fed to animals?
pages.title: The majority of feed from byproducts comes from…

answerExplanation.text:
The byproducts that become animal feed come from many different sources but the biggest one is grains.

Using byproducts for animal feed makes sense if they are inedible for humans. But this is not always the case.

The majority of these grain byproducts come from milling whole grain into refined grain. This is seen as a ‘byproduct’ but you could also see it as food waste because whole grain products have a lot more nutrition that we can eat ourselves.

dailyContent.notificationText: Where does most of the money go? 💰 - Recipe of the day: Cauliflower Oven Pasta

home.title: Where does most of the money go?

pages.title: When we buy food, most of the money goes to supermarkets while many farmers don’t get paid enough 🛒
After supermarkets, where does most of the money go?

answerExplanation.text:
For every euro or dollar we spend on food, 78 cents goes to big corporations. We were surprised by the high share for seed and fertilizer companies. These are the companies that have lots of lobbyists and are promoting ‘efficient farming’ with pesticides and special seeds.

Because of this unequal distribution, paying farmers a fair amount wouldn’t increase the end price of many products that much.

pages.subtitle: The supermarket is super convenient, but let’s try to buy our veggies from a smaller shop or market from time to time.

pages.hometitle: What’s the profit that farmers and supermarkets make?
pages.title: The previous infographic shows where the money goes but it doesn’t include costs and profit 💰
A report in the UK dug into this. For example, they found that the profit that supermarkets make on apples is only 1% 🍎
What’s the profit of the farmer? 🧑🏼‍🌾

answerExplanation.text:
The supermarket, farmer, and distributor all make a very low profit on fresh produce like apples. But while supermarkets can make a profit by selling other items, the farmers face a much bigger risk. Again, paying farmers a fair price wouldn’t increase the end price that much.

What can you do? Try to shop at smaller, organic shops from time to time. The report concludes that organic apples and carrots, sold at alternative markets, provide farmers with a much higher profit.

dailyContent.notificationText: The footprint of plant-based meat 🍔 - Recipe of the day: Pita breads with Mushrooms

home.title: The footprint of plant-based meat

pages.title: There are more and more plant-based alternatives for meat and they definitely have a lower footprint 🍔
But how do they compare to the other plant-based sources of protein? 🫘
Compared to legumes, the climate footprint of plant-based meat is…

answerExplanation.text: Plant-based meat has half the footprint of chicken. Legumes and nuts have an even lower footprint but plant-based meat is a great solution for some of our favorite snacks, like hamburgers or hot dogs.

pages.subtitle: Mushrooms are a great way to replace the taste of meat but for these pita breads you could also use plant-based shoarma.

pages.hometitle: Which processed foods increase the risk of cancer?
pages.title: A large research found that people who eat more ultra-processed foods have a higher chance of cancer and other diseases ☠️
This was especially true for processed meat 🥓
Which other processed product increased the risk of cancer?

answerExplanation.text:
Plant-based alternatives are not associated with cancer, but processed meat is. A plant-based alternative is almost certainly healthier than processed meat like a schnitzel, sausage, bacon, salami, or fast-food burger.

Most plant-based alternatives have fewer calories and less saturated fat. They also don’t contain a lot of added sugars. If you want to choose a healthy plant-based option, look at the salt content: it should be 1,1 g or less (per 100g).

dailyContent.notificationText: The new normal of flying insects 🐝 - Recipe of the day: One-pot Lentil Rice

home.title: The new normal of flying insects

pages.title: Because of climate change, pesticides, and other issues, the whole community of flying insects is in trouble 🐝
A study in Germany found that the number of flying insects decreased even in protected areas 🦋
How much did flying insects decrease?
pages.subtitle: total biomass in the past 27 years in protected areas in Germany

answerExplanation.text:
27 years is a very short time, but not in the life of a human. If you’re born in the 90s (like us), you don’t even know what the ‘normal’ amount of insects feels like. That makes it difficult to compare and feel the extreme changes.

The decrease of insects is a serious issue for food production but also for wildlife. One way to help them: take a patch of garden and leave it alone. Just let nature do its thing!

pages.subtitle: The cashews we use in this recipe would no longer exist if we lose all pollinating insects.

pages.hometitle: Can improving a small patch of greenery make a difference for insects?
pages.title: In 2016, scientists in Melbourne counted insects on a small patch of green surrounded by roads and high buildings 🌆
It was no larger than a tennis court and had some grass and two trees 🌳
Then they transformed it with new soil and added 12 native plant species 🌿
What happened after one year?

answerExplanation.text:
After only one year, there were already 5 times more insects species. After 3 years, three of the plant species had died but still, insect species had increased 7-fold.

If you can influence a small area of land - in your own garden or together with others - it can make a big difference for insects.

dailyContent.notificationText: Cows with two ‘lives’ 🐄 - Recipe of the day: Sweet Potato Mash with Sauerkraut

home.title: Cows with two ‘lives’

pages.title: There are two ways to get beef: from cows that are specifically held for meat. Or from dairy cows that no longer give milk 🐄
Meat from dairy cows has a much lower footprint, but there is a downside.
What’s the downside of meat from dairy cows?

answerOptions.title: You can’t get a steak from it 🥩

answerExplanation.text:
Meat from dairy cows has a lower footprint, but you can’t get the luxury cuts like a steak. We’re often cherry-picking the best cuts of meat which only come from beef cows. If we want to have more sustainable meat, we also need to eat the other parts, for example sausage, minced meat, and chewy meats.

If you still want to eat beef, go to a butcher and ask for meat from ‘dual purpose’ cows. These are breeds of cows that give both high-quality milk and beef. They are healthier cows but also rare, because they are seen as less productive.

pages.subtitle: Sauerkraut works really well with sausages… but this sauerkraut recipe is also delicious without any meat!

pages.hometitle: Are traditional cow breeds more sustainable?
pages.title: Today most cows are specialized: either they are for milk OR beef production 🐄
But there are also traditional all-round cow breeds that are specialized for a specific ecosystem 🌿
Compared to special dairy cows, these all-round cows…

answerOptions.title: Need to eat soy to produce enough milk 🥛

answerExplanation.text:
Many traditional breeds produce less milk but have other benefits: they only require grass (instead of also needing soy) and can survive outside all winter.

Often, these cows also help with promoting biodiversity. For example, there is a specific type of cow that is perfect for maintaining heathland. They eat just the right type of plants and also spread seeds in their fur.

dailyContent.notificationText: Gas vs. induction 🍳 - Recipe of the day: Zucchini Peach Wraps

home.title: Gas vs. induction

pages.title: It’s time to take a look at the energy we use in our own home for cooking food.
Cooking on gas causes about 75 kg CO2 per year. How much is it with induction?
pages.subtitle: with current numbers from the Netherlands

answerExplanation.text: Right now the saved emissions are very little because most electricity is generated with fossil fuel. But even when the electricity comes from renewable sources, it’s much more important to look at what we eat. The average Dutch diet has a footprint of around 1300 kg CO2e per person.

pages.subtitle: We love induction cooking, but if you have a gas stove: place wraps directly on the flame for 20-30 seconds and make them really delicious.

pages.hometitle: What is another benefit of cooking on induction?
pages.title: While we were researching this topic we found another interesting benefit of induction 👀
What is less likely when cooking on induction?
pages.subtitle: compared to gas

answerExplanation.text:
Cooking on gas causes indoor air pollution. A study found that children living in households that use gas stoves for cooking are 42% more likely to have asthma. Ventilation and air purifiers can help but the best option is cooking electrically.

While gas is not the cleanest option, it would still be a huge improvement (for health & climate) for the millions of people currently cooking on wood or animal dung. That's why 'Clean Cooking' also made it to the list of Project Drawdown, one of our favorite resources about climate solutions.

dailyContent.notificationTitle: Today: la Prateria VerdeMoo Moo Meadows
dailyContent.notificationText: What is a sustainable amount of dairy? 🥛 - Recipe of the day: Creamy Beetroot Rice

home.title: What is a sustainable amount of dairy?

pages.title: In a story at Muscle Mountain we already saw that a little bit of dairy can be part of a sustainable diet 🐄
The recommended amount in the EAT-Lancet report is 250 ml of whole milk equivalents 🥛
Whole milk equivalents stands for the amount of milk that went into a dairy product like cream or cheese 🧀
What is the equivalent of 250 ml of whole milk?

answerExplanation.text: 250 ml of whole milk equivalents is about one portion of any dairy product. Overall, we can say we need to bring back our dairy consumption from 2-3 portions to 1 portion per day. This recommendation is not a hard truth but a guideline.

pages.subtitle: A rice recipe with (soy) cream!

pages.hometitle: Who should eat more cheese, men or women?
pages.title: The WWF used a similar approach to determine a sustainable amount of dairy. In their version, there is an interesting difference between men and women 👫
Compared to men, women should eat… 🧀

answerExplanation.text:
In the WWF calculations, women should eat more cheese (and red meat) than men. Women get 147 g per week and men only 21 g of cheese! That’s because they have higher requirements for iron due to blood loss during menstruation.

The poster we created (see Supporter page) shows all the amounts, but we used the average for men and women.

dailyContent.notificationText: What’s the impact of your favorite drink? 🍺 - Recipe of the day: Noodles with Orange Juice

home.title: What’s the impact of your favorite drink?

pages.title: So far we’ve mostly looked at the impact of food. But drinks also require land and have a footprint 🍾
For example, to make 1 liter of beer you need around 200 grams of barley 🌾
During a night out, beers often go together with fast food 🍔 So how do they compare?
How many beers have the same impact as one chicken burger? 🍺

answerExplanation.text:
The impact of a drink depends on our habits: the emissions of milk and coffee can really add up if you drink it every day. In that case, trying to reduce coffee or switching to plant-based milk can make a difference.

There are lots of reasons to drink less alcohol but the footprint of the occasional beer is relatively low. The snacks after a night out probably have a higher impact: one chicken burger has the same footprint as 7 beers. 🍻 (47 in the case of beef)

pages.subtitle: In this recipe the broccoli soaks up the flavors of the orange juice. Delicious!

pages.hometitle: What characterizes the climate impact of wine and beer?
pages.title: The carbon footprint from food usually comes from growing the food 🌾 But for beer and wine it’s different
Which part of the carbon footprint plays a relatively big role?
pages.subtitle: for wine & beer

answerExplanation.text:
Wine and beer usually come in glass, which creates higher packaging emissions. And transport emissions are high because both the glass and all the water are heavy.

But even then it’s not something to worry about too much: transporting a bottle of wine from Australia to the UK still has a lower footprint than driving a car to a local winery yourself that’s 5 km away.

dailyContent.notificationText: Is grass-fed beef more sustainable? 🥩 - Recipe of the day: Spinach Pesto

home.title: Is grass-fed beef more sustainable?

pages.title: Many people say that the solution for meat is eating grass-fed beef 🥩
There are many reasons to feed cows grass instead of corn, but a lower climate impact is not one of them 🐂
Why does grass-fed beef not have a lower footprint? 🥩

answerOptions.title: It requires more cows for the same amount of meat
Most grass-fed beef is shipped form Australia to the US/Europe

answerExplanation.text:
Cows that only eat grass are healthier, but they also grow slower. So you need more cows for the same amount of meat. And this requires extra land and causes extra methane emissions.

Beef has a high footprint because: 1) cows burp methane 2) cows require a lot of feed. Whether you get this feed from grain or grass doesn’t change the fact that it requires a lot of land, which has a high carbon cost.

There are other benefits of grazing cows but not for the climate: while cows can help grasslands to store carbon, this does not offset the extra emissions.

pages.subtitle: This pasta recipe is a great alternative to the traditional bolognese (which requires minced beef)

pages.hometitle: How heavy are today’s cows compared to the bison that used to roam the prairie?
pages.title: Sometimes people say that cows are just like bison that used to roam the prairie 🦬
So we were curious: how much did all wild mammals weigh before humans took over the planet? 🦣
Compared to all of today’s cows, all wild mammals weighed… 🦣
pages.subtitle: 100.000 years ago

answerExplanation.text: All the mammoths, rhinos, bison, etc. combined still weighed 3 times less than today’s cows! Another reminder that we need to eat a lot less beef and dairy.

dailyContent.notificationText: How much do you spend on food? 🤑 - Recipe of the day: Coconut Dahl

home.title: How much do you spend on food?

pages.title: The price of food is a topic of many conversations with the current inflation 📈
So let’s take a look at how much we spend on food 🤑
How much of their income do people in the Netherlands spend on food? 💰
pages.subtitle: as percentage of household income in 2018

answerExplanation.text:
In rich Western countries we only spend around 6-12% on food. But Dutch people think it’s three times higher!

Eating sustainably is often cheaper because meat is expensive. But we also need to make farming more sustainable and fair and buying those products is often more expensive at the moment. So if you can allow it, don’t be too stingy with food.

pages.subtitle: The Fairtrade versions of products like coconut milk are an example of where spending the extra money is worth it.

pages.hometitle: Is a sustainable diet more or less expensive?
pages.title: Scientists calculated how much it would cost to switch to a sustainable & healthy diet 💰
A healthy & sustainable diet is…
pages.subtitle: in high-income countries

answerExplanation.text:
In high-income countries, a healthy & sustainable diet with lots of veggies and legumes and low amounts of meat & dairy is cheaper than the current diet. The model shows that eating plant-based can even reduce costs by one-third!

These diets would cost more in low-income countries, but the typical Western diet is even more expensive.

dailyContent.notificationText: Is honey sustainable? 🍯 - Recipe of the day: Celeriac Sweet Potato Mash

home.title: Is honey sustainable?

pages.title: There are over 16,000 known bee species in the world but only 8 of them are considered ‘honey bees’ 🐝
While most insects are in decline, honey bees have increased the last 50 years 📈 What does it mean for the pollination of food?
Which bees are better at pollinating food crops?

answerExplanation.text:
The reason that we have so many honey bees is because we use them as ‘honey farms’. While honey bees are really good at collecting pollen for honey, they don’t stop at other flowers to pollinate them. And if there are too bee hives in one area, there is no more food for other pollinators.
